summary: The agent identifies that connective tissue grafts provide no predictable gain in Class III recessions—corresponding to severe defects like Cairo RT-3—which makes graft placement ineffective there compared to milder forms such as Classes I or II where success rates improve significantly. Thus, graft placement fails specifically in Class III recession due to its inherent severity exceeding treatment efficacy limits.
